This is likely related to their anti-cheat, and is an intentional decision on their side to not allow these games to launch on Linux-based Platforms.

As a note Steam Deck does not come with Secure Boot enabled at all.

This is not a bug.

Just refund to Valve, and boycott to EA.

There are great games out there

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Dread X Collection: The Hunt
- Steam AppID of the game: 1569550

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 5700
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 23.0.0-git
- Kernel version: 5.17.9-302.fsync.fc36.x86_64
- Proton Version: proton-7.0-2

[system_info.txt](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8794360/system_info.txt)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms

Dread X Collection: The Hunt is a collection of short games.

"Axis Mundi" has a corrupt intro video #1464

"The Fruit" crashes the display driver, unable to recover and having to do a hard reset (I did see that someone else on ProtonDB with AMD GPU has reported a similar thing). 

## Reproduction

Axis Mundi:
- Select Start Game > Free Play Mode
- Select the "PH" report on the board to launch "Axis Mundi"
![Screenshot_20220529_225059](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43387963/170892437-d2dd8328-b11d-4714-968c-d85b3bdc6254.png)
- Start new game, and you will see a corrupted intro video:
![Screenshot_20220529_225209](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43387963/170892469-6331f3b9-2ff0-459d-8b69-7ec02e61e277.png)
[steam-1569550-AxisMundi.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8794359/steam-1569550-AxisMundi.log)

I was able to fix the video by setting launch option
"WINE_DO_NOT_CREATE_DXGI_DEVICE_MANAGER=1 %command%"

The Fruit:
- Select Start Game > Free Play Mode
- Select report on the board to launch "The Fruit"
- Start the game
- Bring up the lantern (press 4) and walk forward until you reach a bridge, after the bridge enter the shed to the left. Move the barrels and look at the picture hidden behind one of them. Read the note on the table. Exit the shed and follow the road forward (it will start raining). The display driver will crash just before you reach a village. 

Note that triggering the crash appears to be very specific (I have been able to replicate the problem three times in a row doing the steps above), if you just walk forward (without taking the detour entering the shed etc..). The driver does not crash. 

Log of playing the Fruit until it crashes:
[steam-1569550-fruit.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8794358/steam-1569550-fruit.log)

following_discussion.discussion_body:
#5881 Proton-7.0-3-rc1 appears to have fixed both the issues I reported :tada: 

Axis Mundi - intro video now looks correct
The Fruit - I made three attempts to instigate the driver crash, and passed without issue 

I'm closing this for now as I'm no longer seeing problems. I have seen an older report on ProtonDB about missing content in the "Rose of Meat" episode, but I have not spotted any problem myself so far.

Including logs of playing the games beyond where the problems happened:
[steam-1569550-fruit.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8835376/steam-1569550-fruit.log)
[steam-1569550-hunt.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8835377/steam-1569550-hunt.log)
[system_info.txt](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8835378/system_info.txt)

following_discussion.discussion_body:
I have a different issue with this game and was told to post it here.

EDIT : Thanks to @JacobSvenningsen I also upgraded Mesa to 25.1.2 and the issue seems to have disappeared !

## System Information
OS : Debian Trixie (13)
CPU : AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D
GPU : AMD RX 7800 XT
Mesa version : 25.0.5 [Issue fixed with 25.1.2]
Kernel version : 6.12.27
Proton version : Tested with 9.0.4, Experimental and Hotfix
Game version : 1.3.2 (Latest)
No mods used
[Steam Runtime System Information](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Seud/dacfb72541f6ff2d42446d6b985eb4d6/raw/757391f7be9dbf8a4fd1603994fc95b6bdca3c16/gistfile1.txt)

## Description

I purchased the game around the end of April and enjoyed it bug-free for around a week, then I did a system update and since then, every time I load the game, a single PAK file will fail to load entirely. In game this means missing textures and models (showing as white blobs), and occasionally crashes during loading if it hits a critical file.

In the logs, this shows as a plethora of lines with "[ERROR] ReadFile returned 1". But the truly bizarre part of the bug ? Every time the game is launched, the affected PAK file _changes_, meaning a whole new set of corrupted textures. And of course the previously affected PAK file miraculously works again so it's not a matter of corrupted files.

### Affected files (for now)

data\characters.pak
data\hlod_prefab.pak
data\ipl_characters-part2.pak
data\ipl_characters-part3.pak
data\ipl_objects-part0.pak
data\ipl_objects-part1.pak
data\ipl_objects-part3.pak
data\ipl_objects-part4.pak

## Reproduction

Start a new game or load a save

## Things I tried

- System updates
- Validating game files (including deleting all PAK files to force re-download)
- Purging shader cache

## Examples

Launch No 1 (Notice the missing texture on the guard)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67343f1b-0edc-44e7-bf74-c1b74d9a6655)
[Game log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20639739/kcd.Build.0.06.Jun.25.10.11.02.log)

Launch No 2 (Notice the missing texture for the road, buildings and vegetation in the center)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5d9ead27-116f-4bd8-a9f0-d72c0401b739)
[Game log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20639740/kcd.Build.0.06.Jun.25.10.15.04.log)

## Additional files

[Proton log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20639738/steam-1771300.log)
[DPKG log of the related update](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20639737/dpkg-kcd.log)

Managed to resolve it.
Went ahead and followed some bad practice and installed the mesa packages from [Kisak PPA](https://launchpad.net/~kisak/+archive/ubuntu/kisak-mesa?field.series_filter=oracular), bringing Mesa to 25.1.2
Currently it's working using that version of Mesa and using Proton 10.0-1 (beta).


# Original post:

I have a different issue which prevents the game from working altogether.

# System Information
OS : Debian Sid
CPU : AMD Ryzen 9 7900X3D
GPU : AMD RX 7800 XT
Mesa version : Initially **25.0.5-2**. Then upgraded to **25.1.0-1**
Kernel version : Initially **6.12.30-1**. Then tried upgrading to **6.15.1**
Proton version : Tested with 9.0.4, 10.0-1 (beta), Experimental, Hotfix and even Glorious Eggroll 10-4
Game version : 1.3.2 (Latest)
[System diagnostics](https://gist.github.com/JacobSvenningsen/81d5a9622fb64f2806e4754460a7b88e)

## Description

Back in February and March, I played the game extensively with no real issue, but trying to run the game again yesterday, I'm greeted with one of three outcomes:
* The splashscreen shows up and nothing more happens
* A black screen pops up. Alt-tabbing reveals the splashscreen is still present
* Intro logos are being displayed and as soon as the EULA, DLC screen information, or the main menu is reached, it freezes.

When the last outcome occurs, the music is still playing and the cursor can be moved. Pressing one of the buttons, plays a sound which could indicate that the button was actually pressed.
It feels driver related, and it might not be Proton specific. Scouring the internet, it seems Windows users are reporting similar issues, which sometimes are reported to be fixed by a reboot, removing the game's shader cache or a driver update. Deleting the game's shader cache can lead to the last outcome (freeze at main menu) to become more likely, but doesn't resolve anything.

Tried upgrading mesa, the kernel, tried different proton version and reinstalling the game to another drive. 

## Additional files
[Log when the main menu is reached](https://gist.github.com/JacobSvenningsen/4fc30b3a463237241cbf6d6bc46f5c32)
[vulkaninfo](https://gist.github.com/JacobSvenningsen/d7858e78f9e3d3fd00b21e5b7d51d45a)
[kcd.log](https://gist.github.com/JacobSvenningsen/a25c34ae131a60ff325061cd3c72575a)
[kcd_launcher.log](https://gist.github.com/JacobSvenningsen/9d68f1ccac705a7882654be4afa1f2ca)

> Managed to resolve it. Went ahead and followed some bad practice and installed the mesa packages from [Kisak PPA](https://launchpad.net/~kisak/+archive/ubuntu/kisak-mesa?field.series_filter=oracular), bringing Mesa to 25.1.2 Currently it's working using that version of Mesa and using Proton 10.0-1 (beta).

Holy crap you just fixed my issue too ! Looks like it is a problem with Mesa 25.0 then.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
@ipr Hi there. Is the game installed in the default location on your local drive and which filesystem do you use?

Does the below command help getting any further in the classic modes?
```
PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%
```

> @ipr Hi there. Is the game installed in the default location on your local drive and which filesystem do you use?
> 
> Does the below command help getting any further in the classic modes?
> 
> ```
> PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%
> ```

With this the fullscreen-version works same way as the windowed version: it opens a window and then stops with black screen. Without it fullscreen-version does not do even that.

Filesystem is ext4 so that shouldn't be a problem, it is not "external" drive either (not a removable drive, internally mounted) if that is what you are asking.
Various other games on same drive are working fine so it isn't that.
Looks like there is opt-in beta-version for that renpy-made version. So after opting in for that the TL_renpy version works.
